claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children actually - Alex is 7, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7. They're all living at home with me. I live in Manchester, by the way.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have three children - Alex (7), Blake (20), and Charlie (7), all living at home with you in Manchester.

Let me ask a follow-up question about Blake, who is 20 years old: **Is Blake currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training, but generally stops at age 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in education or training. He actually works full-time now - has a job at a local warehouse.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, he would not qualify for Child Benefit (as it generally stops at age 20, or when a child turns 20 if they were in approved education or training).

Now, regarding Alex and Charlie who are both 7 years old - let me ask: **Are you responsible for their day-to-day care? That is, do they live with you full-time, and are you the person who provides for their needs like food, clothes, and general care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. They both live with me full-time and I provide for all their needs - food, clothes, school things, everything they need really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know. 

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?** (Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Do you live in the UK?** (I know you mentioned you live in Manchester, but I just need to confirm you're residing in the UK to complete the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I live in the UK, here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

Based on the information you've provided, here's your Child Benefit eligibility:

✅ **Alex (age 7)**: You ARE eligible for Child Benefit
✅ **Charlie (age 7)**: You ARE eligible for Child Benefit  
❌ **Blake (age 20)**: You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit

**Summary:**
You can claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Charlie as they are under 16, living with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care. Nobody else is claiming for them, so you meet all the eligibility requirements.

However, Blake does not qualify because he is 20 years old. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 and working full-time, he falls outside the age criteria.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their care and provide for all their needs (food, clothes, school supplies), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 and working full-time, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Charlie is 7 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their care and provide for all their needs (food, clothes, school supplies), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.

Blake, at 20 years old and working full-time (not in approved education or training), does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie.

Charlie is 7 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their care and provide for all their needs (food, clothes, school supplies), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.
